name: Dormer window
description: A vertical window-housing that projects outward from a sloped roof plane, carving usable headroom and daylight into an attic floor that would otherwise be hemmed in by the rafters. The dormer reads as a small box pushed through the roof: a short vertical wall section frames a 1-2 wide by 2 tall window, flanked by cheek walls tying back into the main roof, and crowned by its own pitched mini-roof (gable, hip, or shed). The cladding matches the main roof material so the dormer reads as carved out of the same shell rather than bolted on. Typically arranged in a rhythmic row of 1-3 per facade, dormers turn dead loft into a habitable upper room while preserving the sheltering silhouette of the house.
